title: Natural Born Kissers

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Car screeching sound indicates Homer crashed into something
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation after yet another mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase with perfect character integration, though not particularly original at this point in the series

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Look, honey, I clipped on my tie all by myself.
jokes.setup: Adult man proudly announcing basic task completion
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Homer being proud of something so simple
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's childlike pride in mundane accomplishments

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm going to the store to buy some beer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! I forgot my wallet... But I went to the store anyway!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And I came back home! Woo-hoo!

jokes.text: Hey, look what was in here! A program from that guy's funeral.
jokes.setup: Finding something nostalgic in a suit pocket
jokes.punchline: It's a funeral program for someone he can barely remember
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about forgetting someone who died, with good character insight about Homer's callousness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh boy, a funeral program! Let me see who died... Uh, who's 'Gerald Samson'?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that was your college roommate.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah... or was he? I have no idea who that is.

jokes.text: You mean Frank Grimes? - Yeah, him. Whatever happened to that guy?
jokes.setup: Casual inquiry about someone's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: They're discussing a man whose funeral program they just found
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark callback that shows Homer's complete obliviousness to the impact of Grimes' death

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, whose funeral program is this?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't know. I found it in my jacket pocket.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, it says here he died in 1987.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh. So he's... still dead?

jokes.text: What are you doing? - Playing Hot Wheels. Ow! That had a guide pin in it.
jokes.setup: Question about what appears to be innocent play
jokes.punchline: Bart is literally throwing toy cars at people
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations with Bart's typical destructive interpretation of games

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Playing with my toy cars.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: It looks like you're throwing them at people.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I am throwing them at people.

jokes.text: I'm not Bart. I'm Rod Flanders. - There you go with that smart mouth!
jokes.setup: Child politely correcting an adult's mistake
jokes.punchline: Grampa's hostile reaction to basic factual correction
jokes.explanation: Shows Grampa's cantankerous nature and confusion in a believable way

dialogue.speaker: Rod Flanders
dialogue.line: Actually, sir, that's not quite right. The capital of France is Paris, not Pairs.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Why you little! Don't you dare correct me, you pious little weasel! I fought in World War Two! I know where the dang capital of France is!

jokes.text: Lisa, run outside and cut me a switch. Yes, sir!
jokes.setup: Grampa treating the kids like it's the old days
jokes.punchline: Lisa cheerfully agrees to help him get a switch to beat her with
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about old-fashioned child discipline with Lisa's innocent compliance

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: Now listen here, if you kids don't behave, I'm gonna have to get a switch and tan your hides!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'll help you find one, Grampa!

jokes.text: Boring! I'll take you to a place that's really romantic.
jokes.setup: Child promising to show parents something romantic
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Bart being a romance expert
jokes.explanation: Sets up the punchline of where Bart would consider romantic

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey Mom and Dad, I want to show you guys something really romantic.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Romantic? Since when does Bart know anything about romance?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba, Dad, I know plenty about romance!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's sweet, honey, but what could you possibly teach us?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, first you gotta compliment her on her appearance...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey, that's not a bad idea. Marge, you look... pink.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: No, Dad, you gotta be more specific!

jokes.text: you'll see Homer and Marge 'Sampson' who are celebrating with us today their 11th air-niversary.
jokes.setup: Professional announcement about celebrating passengers
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'air-niversary' and mispronounced name
jokes.explanation: Mild pun humor with additional awkwardness of name mispronunciation

dialogue.speaker: Captain
dialogue.line: Ladies and gentlemen, we're celebrating our air-niversary!

dialogue.speaker: Captain
dialogue.line: That's right, it's been one year since we've had a crash!

jokes.text: Hey, Jose, easy up, huh? - Sorry. We were only hired to park cars.
jokes.setup: Complaint about rough airplane handling
jokes.punchline: The pilot isn't actually a pilot, just valet parking
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd escalation explaining the terrible flying - classic Simpsons logic

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This plane is being flown like a madman! Who's flying this thing?

dialogue.speaker: Pilot
dialogue.line: I am, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, you're doing a terrible job!

dialogue.speaker: Pilot
dialogue.line: Actually, I'm just the valet. The pilot called in sick. I parked your car once, so I figured I could handle this.

jokes.text: You mean there's been cake in our freezer for 11 years? Why was I not informed?
jokes.setup: Discovery of old wedding cake
jokes.punchline: Homer's outrage at missing out on cake rather than sentimentality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment prioritizing food over romance with great character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're telling me there's been a perfectly good wedding cake sitting in the freezer this whole time and nobody thought to tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, it's been there since our wedding day. We were saving it for a special occasion.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A special occasion?! The only special occasion I need is cake in my mouth! And now it's all freezer burnt and gross. Do you have any idea what you've done to me?

jokes.text: Hey, Marge, wouldn't it be weird if they had little parties at night? - Wee little parties?
jokes.setup: Looking at wedding cake figurines
jokes.punchline: Homer imagining tiny plastic people having parties
jokes.explanation: Charming absurd thought that shows Homer's childlike imagination

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Look at them, Marge. They're so happy. I bet they're having the time of their lives up there.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, they're made of plastic.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So? That doesn't mean they can't party. In fact, I bet they're partying right now. Look, the groom is doing a keg stand!

jokes.text: you know, 'rock the casbah.' - Yeah, it seems like the thing to do.
jokes.setup: Married couple trying to initiate intimacy
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemism that sounds more like duty than passion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how routine their intimacy has become

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey Marge, wanna... you know... do the thing?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's the least romantic thing you've ever said.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, what do you want me to say?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Something that makes me feel special, not like I'm on your to-do list!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Fine. Marge, will you please participate in marital relations at your earliest convenience?

jokes.text: So, are you gonna- Oh. Did you want me to? Oh, no. No, I'm the guy.
jokes.setup: Continuing awkward pre-sex negotiation
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused traditional gender role confusion
jokes.explanation: Realistic cringe comedy about long-married couple awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I want you to take charge tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Take charge? But honey, you're the woman. Isn't that your job?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: No, Homer. I want you to be more assertive and romantic.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Assertive? I thought that's what I do when I burp the alphabet!

jokes.text: Extended awkward intimacy sequence with interruptions and apologies
jokes.setup: Couple attempting to be romantic
jokes.punchline: Series of physical mishaps and awkward positioning
jokes.explanation: Sustained cringe sequence but lacks sharp individual moments

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, tonight's the night. I've got candles, rose petals, and I even shaved my back.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, that's so sweet!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! Ow!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, what's wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I can't get my leg up there. I'm stuck!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Let me help you. Just... a little to the left.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No, your other left! Oof! The bed's on fire!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's the candles, Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Even worse!

jokes.text: Look who's here! - Ooh, who's a good boy? - He's the best boy!
jokes.setup: Interruption during intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Both immediately abandon romance for baby-talking to their dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect representation of how couples prioritize pets over romance

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh Marge, I love you so much.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, me too.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Who's a good boy? Who's a good boy? Yes you are!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's right, you're such a good boy! Such a good boy!

jokes.text: Hey, Marge, wasn't that great when the dog came in here? - Oh, yeah! He's really special!
jokes.setup: Post-romantic moment conversation
jokes.punchline: They're more excited about the dog than their attempted intimacy
jokes.explanation: Sad but funny commentary on their relationship priorities

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, that was...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I know, Homer. I know.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Did you hear that sound?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What sound?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I think Santa's Little Helper wants to go for a walk!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, we were just—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Come on, boy! Let's go!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What? He's been cooped up all day!

jokes.text: Hmm, that's queer. [Groans] - Homer, don't look! - What? Aaah! The food!
jokes.setup: Something seems wrong in the kitchen
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic reaction to spoiled food on the floor
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with Homer's food obsession

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! The meatloaf! My precious meatloaf!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's been on the floor for three days.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Floor?! I thought it was just... aging!

jokes.text: Why did this happen? Why? [Sobbing]
jokes.setup: Appliance breakdown causing food spoilage
jokes.punchline: Homer treating minor inconvenience like major tragedy
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's dramatic overreaction to food loss

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Not the refrigerator! Do you know what this means?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: The food will spoil?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Worse! I'll have to eat things that are room temperature! Room temperature, Marge!

jokes.text: Eh, I never thought I'd live this long.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if this is how he imagined married life
jokes.punchline: Homer's casually dark admission about life expectations
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny one-liner that's very Homer - deflecting with casual fatalism

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Is this how you imagined married life?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No, I figured I'd be dead by now.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you need... a divorce! [Gong Clangs]
jokes.setup: Radio advice about marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Suggesting divorce instead of counseling, with dramatic gong
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with absurd business model for divorce lawyers

dialogue.speaker: Radio Host
dialogue.line: Well, caller, instead of wasting money on counseling, why not just get a divorce?

dialogue.speaker: Radio Host
dialogue.line: It's much cheaper and you'll be miserable alone instead of miserable together.

jokes.text: I'll get us out of this, honey. It's just gonna take a whole lot of floorir.
jokes.setup: Car stuck in mud
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident plan to use more gas (with made-up word)
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical overconfident solution that will obviously make things worse

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Don't worry, I'll get us out of this mud. I just need to use more gas!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Full speed ahead! We'll hydroplaneate right out of here!

jokes.text: And that's why today bananas are called 'yellow fatty beans.'
jokes.setup: Grampa telling story to kids
jokes.punchline: Complete nonsense delivered with authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa Simpson rambling nonsense with confident delivery

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Back in my day, we didn't have fancy things like 'directions' or 'sense.' We just wandered into the woods and whatever happened, happened. Lost my shoe to a bear once. Or was it a very angry mailman? Can't remember. Anyway, I was there for three weeks. Turned out I was in my own backyard the whole time.

jokes.text: Hey, are they pulling the plug on anybody today? - Nope, everybody's paid up.
jokes.setup: Child asking about nursing home activities
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of life support decisions based on payment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about healthcare economics delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: So what do you do all day at the nursing home, Grampa?

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: Well, we sit around and talk about our various ailments. Yesterday, I told everyone about my ingrown toenail for three hours.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: That sounds boring.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: It is! That's why they keep us on life support — so we don't have to do anything else.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, they keep you on life support just so you don't get bored?

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: Well, as long as the Medicare checks keep coming in. The day those stop, they're pulling the plug.

jokes.text: And that's how I earned the Iron Cross.
jokes.setup: Story about war service clearing mines
jokes.punchline: Claiming to have earned a Nazi medal
jokes.explanation: Grampa's confused war stories taking a dark historical turn

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: I earned a Nazi medal for my bravery in clearing mines during the war.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Dad, that doesn't sound right.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, I was very brave!

jokes.text: That's my brass knee. Steel hip. That one's news to me.
jokes.setup: Metal detector beeping on Grampa's body parts
jokes.punchline: Escalating revelation of artificial body parts, ending with one he didn't know about
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure showing Grampa's deterioration and absent-mindedness

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: Well, I've got a metal hip, a metal knee, metal plates in my skull...

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Mr. Simpson, you also have a metal pancreas.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: A metal pancreas? I didn't know about that!

jokes.text: When you think about it, mud is nothing but wet dirt.
jokes.setup: Couple stuck in mud discussing their situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's profound observation about obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Homer treating obvious observation as philosophical insight

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We're stuck in mud.

jokes.text: If somebody's in here, you're in for some serious ass forkin'!
jokes.setup: Farmer threatening trespassers
jokes.punchline: Pitchfork threat with crude pun
jokes.explanation: Low-brow pun humor with rural character stereotype

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: Get off my land or I'll use this pitchfork! You might say I'm about to... fork over some pain!

jokes.text: Huh. Well, maybe they're in the media room.
jokes.setup: Rural farmer searching for intruders
jokes.punchline: Barn having a 'media room' suggests unexpected sophistication
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of rural expectations with modern amenity

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: Who's been in my barn? I found a media room!

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: A media room? In MY barn? What's next, a wine cellar?

jokes.text: Hey, public display! - Yeah, what's with the love thang?
jokes.setup: Kids witnessing parents being affectionate
jokes.punchline: Children's disgusted reaction to parental romance
jokes.explanation: Believable kid reaction to parental affection with good character voices

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ugh, get a room!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Yeah, seriously. We're eating here.

jokes.text: Let's just say the country air did us good.
jokes.setup: Kids asking about parents' newfound affection
jokes.punchline: Homer's coy euphemism for their barn encounter
jokes.explanation: Subtle innuendo that works as family-friendly explanation

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What's with all the lovey-dovey stuff, man?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Yeah, Mom and Dad have been acting really weird.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, kids, your mother and I were just... let's call it 'making hay in the barn.'

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer!

jokes.text: but what if this time we use it to buy things? You know, things we like.
jokes.setup: Pirate crew following burial tradition
jokes.punchline: Logical question about actually using treasure
jokes.explanation: Good logical deconstruction of pirate tropes from TV show

dialogue.speaker: Pirate crew member
dialogue.line: Wait, if we're burying the treasure, aren't we just going to dig it back up later?

dialogue.speaker: Pirate captain
dialogue.line: That's the whole point of burying it!

dialogue.speaker: Pirate crew member
dialogue.line: But then why bury it in the first place?

jokes.text: It's a bottle cap. - Jewel-encrusted?
jokes.setup: Metal detector finding treasure
jokes.punchline: Homer's hopeful question about worthless bottle cap
jokes.explanation: Homer's eternal optimism about finding value in garbage

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Is it gold? Is it silver? Is it a rare bottle cap worth thousands?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, it's just a bottle cap.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So... thousands then?

jokes.text: Sorry, guys. Marge and I are spending the weekend at a bed-and-breakfast.
jokes.setup: Friends inviting Homer out
jokes.punchline: Homer choosing romance over beer with the guys
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation about Homer prioritizing wife over friends

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Come on Homer, let's go to Moe's!

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Yeah, we're buying the first round!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sorry guys, I can't. Marge and I have a date night.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: A date? With your wife?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's right. And nothing's gonna stop me. Not even free beer.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Did he just say no to free beer?

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Homer's in love.

jokes.text: Nah, it'd just be awkward, what with the sex and all.
jokes.setup: Barney asking to come along to romantic getaway
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt explanation of why he can't come
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic blunt honesty about inappropriate topics

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, can I come along on your romantic getaway?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No, Barney. This is for me and Marge.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Aw, come on! It'll be fun!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Barney, you're a drunk and you smell like a distillery. Plus, you'd probably hit on Marge, and I'd have to hurt you. So... no.

jokes.text: Yeah, I always figured Marge would be a dynamo in the sack, you know? Oh, boy, she's got legs from here to ya-ya!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Homer's romantic plans
jokes.punchline: Barney's inappropriate sexual commentary about Marge
jokes.explanation: Crude character humor typical of Barney but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Hey! Look at that! [Gasps] Oh, good Lord! - [Gasps] - [Screams] I'm so sorry. I saw everything.
jokes.setup: Couple trying to be intimate at B&B
jokes.punchline: Innkeeper accidentally witnessing them
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy setup that pays off later in the episode

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, finally we're alone. Let's make this romantic night one we'll never forget.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, yes. Just us and the sound of the waves...

dialogue.speaker: Innkeeper
dialogue.line: Excuse me, I was just coming in to turn down the— Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Get out!

dialogue.speaker: Innkeeper
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry! I'll just— I'm leaving!

jokes.text: The fear of getting caught is kind of a turn-on.
jokes.setup: Heart racing from being caught
jokes.punchline: Marge discovering her adventurous side
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Marge's hidden wild side emerging

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my God, my heart is racing! I've never done anything like this before!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I... I kind of liked it. Is this what it feels like to be adventurous?

jokes.text: There's that dirty girl I married.
jokes.setup: Marge excited by the thrill of nearly being caught
jokes.punchline: Homer's appreciation for Marge's adventurous side
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's attraction to Marge's wild side

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that was so thrilling! The danger, the risk of being caught!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, and you know what really gets me excited? When you're all dangerous and adventurous like that.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It's like you're a whole new woman. A woman who might actually leave me.

jokes.text: There's that dirty girl I married.
jokes.setup: Marge revealing her adventurous side
jokes.punchline: Homer's pleased recognition of her wilder nature
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Homer appreciating Marge's hidden depths

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, Marge! I didn't know you had it in you!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, there's a lot you don't know about me, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... adventurous Marge. D'oh, I'm the luckiest guy in the world!

jokes.text: Exactly. Now there's nothing left out there but treasure.
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out they've only found trash
jokes.punchline: Bart's flawed logic about treasure hunting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - removing all trash means treasure is left

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, we've only found trash.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: That's what they said about me, and look how I turned out!

jokes.text: Wow! An alternate ending to Casablanca. - Bart, this could be priceless! - Priceless like a mother's love, or the good kind of priceless?
jokes.setup: Discovery of potentially valuable film artifact
jokes.punchline: Bart distinguishing between emotional and monetary value
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line showing Bart's materialistic worldview with perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, this could be worth a fortune! It's a rare film artifact.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba! How much are we talking?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Well, emotionally it's priceless. Historically significant.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, but what about actual money?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: That's... that's not really the point, Bart.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: It's the only point, man.

jokes.text: It hasn't changed since that magical evening when I knocked you up.
jokes.setup: Homer being romantic about their old dating spot
jokes.punchline: Crude description of Bart's conception
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical romantic crudeness - in character but not especially clever

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Remember this spot? This is where we first made out.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, those were the days.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And right over there behind those rocks... that's where Bart was conceived.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What? I'm being romantic!

jokes.text: Yeah. I thought Bart would be born a dimwit! - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about drinking affecting the baby
jokes.punchline: Implication that Bart did turn out to be affected
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Bart's intelligence with good timing on Marge's agreement

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Maybe I should cut back on the drinking while Marge is pregnant. What if it affects the baby?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's very responsible of you.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, well... *takes a sip of beer* ...I'm sure it'll be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: It was not fine.

jokes.text: Look out, Rick! He's packing heat!
jokes.setup: Alternate Casablanca ending playing
jokes.punchline: Classic movie turned into action sequence
jokes.explanation: Good parody of how Hollywood would ruin classic films

dialogue.speaker: Rick
dialogue.line: Of all the gin joints in all the towns in all the world, she walks into mine...

dialogue.speaker: Ilsa
dialogue.line: Rick, we'll always have Paris.

dialogue.speaker: Rick
dialogue.line: Here's looking at you, kid.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This is the lamest movie ever! Where's the action?

dialogue.speaker: Rick
dialogue.line: Of all the gin joints— BLAM BLAM BLAM!

dialogue.speaker: Ilsa
dialogue.line: Rick, no!

dialogue.speaker: Rick
dialogue.line: Here's looking at you, kid! KABOOM!

jokes.text: Aye, aye, aye, where did you get this, ya shrunken old hag, ya? - I'm just a little girl.
jokes.setup: Hollywood executive examining the film
jokes.punchline: Insulting a child in stereotypical producer fashion
jokes.explanation: Good stereotype of rude Hollywood executive, mild impact

dialogue.speaker: Studio Executive
dialogue.line: Kid, you got talent, but you're also fat and ugly. We'll need to fix that before we can green-light your picture.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm not fat or ugly!

dialogue.speaker: Studio Executive
dialogue.line: That's the spirit, kid. Now get out of my office.

jokes.text: We were just dopes in suits, not like today.
jokes.setup: Executive explaining old Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Implies modern executives are better despite evidence to contrary
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on Hollywood's lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Studio Executive
dialogue.line: Back in the old days of Hollywood, we studio executives were corrupt, power-hungry, and willing to do anything for a buck. But that was then. Today's executives are much more ethical and principled.

jokes.text: We tried to tack that happy ending on the picture, 'cause back then, well, studio execs, we were - we were just dopes in suits, not like today.
jokes.setup: Executive explaining old Hollywood mistakes
jokes.punchline: Claiming executives are better now while demonstrating the opposite
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of Hollywood's lack of self-awareness about its own problems

dialogue.speaker: Studio Executive
dialogue.line: We don't make those kinds of mistakes anymore. We've learned from the past.

dialogue.speaker: Studio Executive
dialogue.line: Now we make new, original mistakes.

jokes.text: Rod, you've got small, girlish hands. Reach in and fish it out.
jokes.setup: Golf ball stuck in windmill hole
jokes.punchline: Ned's inappropriate description of his son's hands
jokes.explanation: Mildly uncomfortable parenting moment typical of Ned's awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, I'll be! My son Rod's got hands like a couple of catcher's mitts! Perfect for golf!

dialogue.speaker: Rod Flanders
dialogue.line: Thanks, Dad!

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Now if only he could use those meat hooks to retrieve my golf ball from this infernal windmill.

jokes.text: We're trapped! - Oh, why can't they just play through?
jokes.setup: Naked couple hiding in golf course windmill
jokes.punchline: Homer treating their predicament like a golf etiquette issue
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic prioritizing golf rules over their embarrassing situation

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey! You're supposed to yell 'fore'!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And keep your clothes on during play!

jokes.text: Who cares what it is? Let's monoxide it!
jokes.setup: Discussion about mysterious entity in windmill
jokes.punchline: Moe's immediate solution is lethal gas
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe violence as first resort, using 'monoxide' as verb

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: There's something in that windmill? Well, we'll just gas it out!

jokes.text: Boy, I'll tell ya. [Chuckles] They only come out at night. Or in this case, uh, the daytime.
jokes.setup: Police chief describing naked people behavior
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's confused attempt at wildlife expertise
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence with nature documentary parody

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You see, when people get naked, they're like animals. And when animals get scared, they either fight or they run away.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: So I'm thinking... maybe if we act like a bigger animal, they'll get scared and run away.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: ROOOAAARRR!

jokes.text: That's a shame. He had one day left till retirement.
jokes.setup: Police dog whining/giving up on scent trail
jokes.punchline: Classic cop movie trope applied to police dog
jokes.explanation: Excellent application of cop movie cliche to absurd situation with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: What is it, boy? You've lost the scent?

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, I guess we'll have to call it a day. Good work out there, Rex.

jokes.text: Well, that's what I'm here for. I mean, you're young, successful, you're naked. You want a car with a radio, right?
jokes.setup: Desperate car salesman encountering naked couple
jokes.punchline: Gil's attempt to make a sales pitch despite the circumstances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gil moment - so desperate he'll pitch to naked people on the run

dialogue.speaker: Gil
dialogue.line: Well, hello there, folks! I couldn't help but notice you two lovebirds are in the market for a vehicle! Why, I've got some fantastic deals on pre-owned automobiles that'll make you want to keep your clothes on! I mean, keep them in the car! What I'm trying to say is, this 1987 Buick LeSabre is perfect for romantic getaways, and it's got a spacious back seat for, uh... conversation!

jokes.text: Yeah, the British tabloids will have a field day.
jokes.setup: Discussing potential scandal
jokes.punchline: Marge's oddly specific concern about foreign media coverage
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity about international tabloid interest in Springfield

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I'm worried about what they'll say about us in the foreign newspapers.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Who cares what those people think?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, what if they print something bad about me in the Portuguese papers? I've been working so hard on my image there.

jokes.text: Ah, they stole the balloon! I've been living in there!
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge escape in hot air balloon
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Gil was homeless and living in the balloon
jokes.explanation: Great character detail about Gil's desperation with good escalation

dialogue.speaker: Gil
dialogue.line: Well, folks, looks like you've found my home sweet home! Yeah, I've been living up here in this balloon for three months. Ever since I lost my job at the car dealership. Say, you wouldn't happen to know anyone looking to hire a down-on-his-luck salesman, would ya?

jokes.text: You go up and down, but not side to side, or back in time.
jokes.setup: Homer figuring out balloon controls
jokes.punchline: Includes time travel as a logical flight direction option
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic including time travel as mundane navigation option

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Alright, let's see... up, down, left, right... and... time travel?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, that's not right. Let me try that again.

jokes.text: You mean this thing? - [Screams] No, that was not the thing. [Groans]
jokes.setup: Homer trying to give Marge balloon operating instructions
jokes.punchline: Marge pulls wrong thing causing disaster
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with predictable outcome

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Okay Marge, pull the red string to inflate it.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Like this?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No no no, the OTHER red string!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oops.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Now quickly! Gaze down at God's fabulous parquet floor. Eyes on the floor. Still on the floor.
jokes.setup: Church service interrupted by naked people in balloon
jokes.punchline: Reverend's desperate attempts to redirect congregation's attention
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with believable religious authority response

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Now, as I was saying about the sanctity of marriage—

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Please, everyone, keep your eyes on the altar!

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Hymn number 247! Let us sing loudly!

jokes.text: Dear Lord, look at that blimp! He's hanging from a balloon!
jokes.setup: Spectacle of Homer hanging from balloon
jokes.punchline: Calling Homer a blimp while noting the balloon
jokes.explanation: Basic fat joke without much creativity or character specificity

dialogue.speaker: Crowd member
dialogue.line: Look at the size of that blimp!

dialogue.speaker: Crowd member
dialogue.line: That's not a blimp, that's Homer Simpson!

dialogue.speaker: Crowd member
dialogue.line: Oh, I was talking about Homer.

jokes.text: Why don't you take a picture? It lasts longer! - [Camera Shutters Clicking] - D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer sarcastically suggesting people take pictures
jokes.punchline: Everyone actually does take pictures
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where Homer's sarcasm backfires in modern camera-phone world

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, go ahead, take pictures. I'm sure this will look great on your Instagram.

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: *everyone immediately pulls out phones and starts taking pictures*

jokes.text: Uh, I was just trying to find Dave Barry's column. He's great. He pokes fun at life's little foibles.
jokes.setup: Homer caught reading scandal sheet about himself
jokes.punchline: Claiming to read highbrow columnist instead
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent lie with pretentious vocabulary he doesn't understand

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not reading this trash! I'm reading this important column by a highbrow intellectual.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: See? 'Dear Drunken Oaf' — very sophisticated.

jokes.text: All those eyes... just leering and leering at us.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on the embarrassing public exposure
jokes.punchline: His traumatized description of the crowd's reaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic interpretation of public humiliation, somewhat overwrought

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why'd they have to look? Why'd they all have to look at me?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I was just standing there, minding my own business, and suddenly everyone's staring. The pointing, the laughing, the taking pictures with their phones...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'll never forget the look of disgust on their faces. It's burned into my brain forever.

jokes.text: Huh. They're gonna feel so silly when they realize they forgot us.
jokes.setup: Kids left behind while family goes to miniature golf
jokes.punchline: Bart's understated reaction to being abandoned
jokes.explanation: Good Bart deadpan delivery showing his resignation to family neglect

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: We've been abandoned.

tmdb_overview: Homer and Marge try to spice up their love life and are caught baring it all in public. Bart and Lisa discover an alternate ending to Casablanca.
insight: Middle-aged romance subplot undercuts pacing; cringe carries thin plot across 64 jokes.
